perturbation.original_text: 2.7 "Sponsorship Rights" means the bundle of rights, services and deliverables Allied will provide to Newegg as set out in Schedule 4, which includes the license of, and rights with respect to, Allied Marks granted in Section 5.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.7 "Sponsorship Rights" means the bundle of rights, services and deliverables Allied will provide to Newegg as set out in Schedule 4.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'which includes the license of, and rights with respect to, Allied Marks granted in Section 5', the definition of Sponsorship Rights becomes incomplete. This introduces uncertainty as to whether the use of Allied Marks is actually included in the Sponsorship Rights. This omission contradicts the later reference in Section 4.1 which refers to Section 5.1
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.1 Allied shall provide, by the license granted in Section 5.1 and otherwise as appropriate, to or for the benefit of Newegg, the Sponsorship Rights, including generally providing advertising space in all of Allied's media and participation in Allied's marketing activities relating to the Arena
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the specific inclusion of 'the license of, and rights with respect to, Allied Marks granted in Section 5' from the definition of 'Sponsorship Rights'. This directly contradicts Section 4.1, which explicitly states that Allied shall provide Sponsorship Rights 'by the license granted in Section 5.1'. The omission creates a direct conflict, as it's no longer clear if the license of Allied Marks is included in Sponsorship Rights, making Section 4.1's reference to Section 5.1 uncertain and potentially unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: 2.8 "Technology E-Commerce (or E-Tail)" means the Arena partnership category that Allied is granting to Newegg and is defined as including technology-focused products in categories including computer systems, components, electronics, gaming, networking, office solutions, software & services, automotive and industrial, home and tools, health & sports, and hobbies and toys. For purposes of clarity, this does not include apparel and accessories.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.8 "Technology E-Commerce (or E-Tail)" means the Arena partnership category that Allied is granting to Newegg and is defined as including products. For purposes of clarity, this does not include apparel and accessories.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'technology-focused products in categories including computer systems, components, electronics, gaming, networking, office solutions, software & services, automotive and industrial, home and tools, health & sports, and hobbies and toys' the definition of Technology E-Commerce becomes ambiguous. This omission creates an in-text contradiction with Section 7.1, which implies that Newegg's sponsorship is specific to the technology e-commerce and online retailer categories.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7.1 In all of Allied's actions and publications (in all media and formats) in connection with the marketing and conducting of the Events, where possible and appropriate, Allied shall where reasonably practicable communicate that Newegg is the exclusive sponsor of the Arena for the technology e-commerce and online retailer categories.
perturbation.justification: The changed text broadens the definition of "Technology E-Commerce (or E-Tail)" by removing 'technology-focused products in categories including computer systems, components, electronics, gaming, networking, office solutions, software & services, automotive and industrial, home and tools, health & sports, and hobbies and toys'. This creates a contradiction with Section 7.1, which states "Newegg is the exclusive sponsor of the Arena for the technology e-commerce and online retailer categories". The original definition limited the scope of Newegg's sponsorship to technology-focused products. The altered definition removes this focus and conflicts with the exclusivity granted in Section 7.1 because it is no longer specific to "technology e-commerce".

perturbation.original_text: 8. Refund or Reduction of Sponsorship Fee 8.1 Without limitation of other rights of Newegg under this Agreement, the Parties agree to negotiate a reasonable reduction and, where applicable, the refund of the Sponsorship Fee to reflect any material restriction in the benefit or value of the Sponsorship Rights to Newegg, including as a result of any change in any laws or regulatory provisions which has an adverse impact on the value of the Sponsorship Rights.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. Refund or Reduction of Sponsorship Fee 8.1 The Parties agree to negotiate a reasonable reduction and, where applicable, the refund of the Sponsorship Fee.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'Without limitation of other rights of Newegg under this Agreement, to reflect any material restriction in the benefit or value of the Sponsorship Rights to Newegg, including as a result of any change in any laws or regulatory provisions which has an adverse impact on the value of the Sponsorship Rights' makes the scope and conditions for a refund or reduction undefined. This creates an in-text contradiction because section 14.6 refers to the mechanism in Section 8.1.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 14.6 Upon termination for any reason, Newegg shall, without prejudice to its other rights, be immediately discharged of all obligations to pay any further Sponsorship Fees not yet rendered or to provide any further Sponsorship Benefits that have not already been delivered to Allied. Further, if Sponsorship Fees have been paid in advance, the Sponsorship Fee shall be prorated through the date of termination and Allied shall refund the portion corresponding to the unused period of the Term.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 8.1 provided specific conditions under which a refund or reduction of the Sponsorship Fee would be considered, explicitly referencing restrictions on benefits or value due to changes in laws or regulations, while also preserving Newegg's other rights. By omitting this clause, the changed text in Section 8.1 creates a scenario where the conditions for a refund or reduction are undefined, weakening the section. Section 14.6 references the now-weakened mechanism in Section 8.1 related to the refund of sponsorship fees. This introduces an in-text contradiction because Section 14.6 assumes a clear process for refund calculation as defined by the original Section 8.1, which has been altered by the omission. The changed Section 8.1 no longer provides sufficient clarity for the application of the conditions for prorated refunds in Section 14.6, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.
